name: Pochmann/Clochmann
description: The Pochmann method (also known as Clochmann) is a beginner-friendly Clock solving technique. It solves the puzzle in four phases: front cross, flip, back cross, and corners. This structured approach makes it easy to learn and provides a clear framework for solving the Clock puzzle from start to finish.

steps.name: Front Cross
steps.description: Solve the four edge clocks on the front face to form a cross pattern. Each edge clock is set to the correct time by pressing the appropriate pins and turning the wheels.
tips: Work on one edge clock at a time
Press the correct pin configuration to isolate the edge you are solving
Develop a consistent order for solving the four edges

steps.name: Flip
steps.description: Flip the Clock puzzle over to access the back face. This is a physical step that transitions from front-face solving to back-face solving.
tips: Flip quickly and consistently to save time
Maintain your grip for a smooth transition
Mentally prepare for the back cross while flipping

steps.name: Back Cross
steps.description: Solve the four edge clocks on the back face, forming a cross on this side. The technique mirrors the front cross step but on the opposite face.
tips: Use the same systematic approach as the front cross
Be careful not to disturb the already-solved front face
Pin configurations on the back affect the front — learn the interactions

steps.name: Corners
steps.description: Solve all four corner clocks. Since the crosses on both faces are already solved, the corners can be adjusted using specific pin and wheel combinations.
tips: Corner solving affects both faces simultaneously
Learn the pin patterns that isolate corner clocks
Practice the corner step in isolation to build speed

pros: Easy to learn for Clock beginners
Clear four-phase structure with no ambiguity
Good foundation for learning more advanced Clock methods
cons: Flip adds time compared to no-flip methods
Not competitive at the highest level
Back cross can accidentally disturb front face work
